title: senecio jacobaea subsp. nemorosus
intro: Senecio jacobaea subsp. nemorosus, also known as woodland ragwort, is a subspecies of the perennial plant Senecio jacobaea, which is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a member of the daisy family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate the liver and gallbladder, and to treat conditions such as jaundice and liver disease. The health benefits of woodland ragwort include its potential to support liver function, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, although it should be used with caution due to its potential toxicity. Woodland ragwort can be prepared as a tincture or infusion, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it, as it can interact with certain medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions.
